messages.text: Katya Farnsworth
We are putting in a 30 min pause to pass accumulation
Is the accumulation from cavannas or matrix?
Done!
Cavanna. We ran out of double sided tape to do a quick roll change. Joey is getting  more for us
This is in pieces, correct?

This is what we have found regarding chocolate feet on the caramels. 


-Water table for tunnel 2 was turned off 


-Fans for tunnel 2 were turned off


Due to that the chocolate for the bottomer would not solidify and create feet before entering enrober


-Blower in the Enrober was on and it needs to be off. Only shaker should be used.


We have turned on the cooling table and the fans and turned off the blower. As soon as the line starts up, we will post the results. Curt Willis Justin Garner
Are these settings captured on the CL’s?

This is the current format that is being used for HER caramels (copy from S1). After talking with Curt Willis and looking closer, we do not have anything on CL sheet for tunnels. 
I will be printing out a blank form to fill out, so that we can have accurate information that we can standardize. Justin Garner
These 3 bins were left on the table from previous shift.  Operators were told not to touch them. Do we know what this is about? Joseph Scott Jody Howse

Operator observed that we kept jamming before Cavanna 4. She noticed that the guide rail kept falling and pieces of caramels were all over the floor. We identified that the bolt was loose cause the rail to fall. Bolt was tightened and everything is running well right now

I noticed that for the WIP spec we do not have the WIP box number, the pieces/box, weight of the box, boxes/pallet. Can we please update that to help with production and any confusion. Candon Watt Curt Willis
Katya Farnsworth are you running the 30891-Bulk150?
No
No
After
New Work Order for Sollich 2 – Monday, June 8 Victor Romero Sr., Elizabeth Hernandez, Joseph Scott
Cavanna's are not performing well. We have a lot of build up. I have paused the line to perform a CIL and pass accumulation Justin Garner Curt Willis
After the tunnel changes and Enrober, the product looks good. Justin Garner Curt Willis
We have a fault on Cavanna 3 and belts keep going down. Maintenance is here looking into it
Lolo is reaching out to Chris for help
Cavanna 3 continues to be down. We have multiple errors right now. Machine was power cycled
Thank you for the update
Still no luck with Cavanna 3. Lolo is on the phone with Chris. We are passing accumulation through Cavanna 4 only.
Might be a long shot, but is there a way to get Scott (Cavanna tech) here? Curt Willis
The reject air was turned off as per Chris, but Cavanna still will not run.

He flew out last night.

It sounds like shutting off the reject didn’t work Chris Givens

In contact with Scott and let him know Lolo was going to reach out to him Katya Farnsworth
Lolo is currently on the phone with Scott. Hopefully we can resolve this soon
Yes
Can someone have Lolo call me? He is turn off the wrong reject. It’s the product detection that needs to be turned off. It controls the first set of blow off devices.

Multiple adjustments were made to the Cavanna and machine was powered down again because the jaws would not cut. We are waiting for the printer to start and then we will test the Cavanna again. We have Jon and Lolo here together

Hello Alvaro Gutierrez! Can we get assistance locating item #41198 please? 

There is only 1 LPN available & we are having a hard time locating it. It says it is in location LE32-01A, but it is not there. Nidia Lopez Corea

Sollich 2 has requested this item # & will need them asap. Tiffanee Taylor -Junior Quinonez
We have it. Joey Michael Dudney
Thank you! We will get it moved in the system to ensure accuracy. Katya Farnsworth
We have tried running product through Cavanna 3, but it keep giving us the same error as before Curt Willis

Chris Givens

Katya Farnsworth have they reached back out to Scott?
We're going to start running the WIP product on MM's
What finally worked to get it going?
We're to start up the line; we're running through the product that accumulated.
Are we cold or plugged going to the Conbar?

No the reserve is still good. 

Though we can’t send to the Conbar until you put on the new plug, which has an internal gasket. 

Once the new plug is cleaned we’ll attach it and start sending to the Conbar if the reserve is still in spec.
The pipe is not, not cold.

The reason we had this happen is that there was a plug at the pipe exit to the Conbar. 

We’re coaching the teams on checking before sending caramel to the Conbar.
Katya Farnsworth. Do we know why a cap was put on?
We’re waiting to replace the plug under the reserve before we send caramel. Maintenance is completing this now.
Caramel is now at the Conbar
We finished processing the WIP with today's date and are changing the date on the packaging to start processing yesterday's WIP in MM's.
The product has already arrived at Cavannas, and maintenance is making adjustments.
We are going to start running the WIP on MMs without box labels.
No. I wasn't aware of that. I will follow up on Monday with  the operators
There is some confusion regarding the dates; QA is reviewing the matter, and the label has not yet been approved for NetSuite to print them.
We had a confusion, both IMS and I, and the labels on the boxes for item 30892 of MM678 are incorrect in the item. We have 6 pallets to rework them. Conner Boyd Elizabeth Hernandez

When we start doing the line clearance for S2 from HER to FD, we’ll send a couple employees to re-label these pallets 
Angelica Perez Elizabeth Hernandez
I’ll ask warehouse to set them aside for now so they don’t get confused amongst the ones with the correct label.
Maintenance is working at Cavanna 3; we already have 30 containers of product accumulated.
We're going to take a pause so we can pass the product around.

Correct label 


 





Incorrect label
I identified them with a Tag so that Warehouse wouldn't move them
Thank you so much

Maintenance is still working on Cavanna 3 and we are only transferring the accumulated product from Cavanna 4. This makes the transfer process slower; we still have 22 containers of accumulated product.
Chris Givens and maintenance is working on the Cavannas. Once we’re down to 5 accumulation bins we’ll begin sending caramel again.

Accumulation is passed (under 5 bins).
Caramel is being sent to the Conbar now.
It seems to be running well, the OMAC alarm hasn't returned. We will keep an eye on it
Turned off the reject sensor, adjusted film and jaw position, we lowered the speed 700 to 600
The gasket is blown out of the valve, we will need to replace it. The cap solution only produced another blow out
We will need to replace that valve gasket whenever we are able to
We have finished the HER pouches job, and we still have a pallet of WIP product. Do you want us to overproduce the job, or is there a way to pack that WIP pallet in bulk?
Curt Willis Conner Boyd, Angelica Perez
I don't know if QA will approve that.
There are 13,500 pieces.
We are already filling melting tank #1 with chocolate 10227, it is already at 60%

For the Bulk150 job, we currently have 675 boxes and need 1,440 to complete the order; however, maintenance is still working on the Cavannas, and we currently have a backlog of 22 containers of product.

Approval has been given to over produce (30892) pouches. 

This will put us ~900 pouches over.
We're going to take another pause topass the accumulated product.

The Cavannas are both up and running well. We have 11/18 pallets complete now. 

We have 32 bins of accumulation. Once those are passed we’ll get a better idea of how much longer we need to send caramel.
Before the pause, with both Cavannas running, time to complete a pallet was 15min.
The WIP product has been fully processed; we are going to return the material and clear the area of ​​any product.

We’re at 14/18 pallets with less than 5 bins of accumulations. 

We sent caramel for 40 minute, due to the Hershey chocolate levels only permitting such (melting tank 18% and buffer 28%) 

This will put us over the 18 needed for WO.

We have 1,357 cases of Bulk150; we are 83 cases short of completing the order. There is another 20 minutes' worth of product coming through the tunnels, so that should complete the order; the third shift needs to process the incoming product.
We have reworked the 6 pallets that had incorrect labels.
Caramel is cooking; reserve is at 67%. After running FD; here is the gasket right under, and a little to the left, S2 reserve tank.

Cycle count FW hersheys 





Thanks Russell Smith
Once the gasket on the kettle is changed we will be starting starting the line soon (12:45a)

Mm6 would not work correctly. We had maintenance working with us all night. A sensor is not fuctioning correctly on top. Justin Garner Chris Givens before production Monday it will need to be looked at

We were having problems with all 3 matrixes especially mm6 causing lots of accumulation and scrap and pouch scrap. Despite lots of help and trying, Maintenance could not help get them running properly. With all the problems I decided to stop the line early and put the caramel that was already on the line into rework buckets.
Alan Todd Alex Mendoza
Alan Todd Alex Mendoza
Alan Todd Alex Mendoza

Mathew Draper,

Did you notice anything different as we had a higher amount of high weights?
I watched but I didn’t notice anything different
For a moment it started to reject high weights, but it stopped now.
Were they actually all high weights?
I’m going through them now
It was reviewed with QA and everything was at its normal weight.
All the high weight bags went through a second time just fine
This belt was moved a bit to the south so I moved it back in line

Among the supersacks brought for the Lot 61AUC line, there is one with two different lot labels; it is being placed on hold because an inspection revealed the product actually belongs to Lot 61BUC. The entire supersack must be inspected.
Mathew Draper Alan Todd

We have a super sack with two different lots inside according to the IMS labels. 

Alan Todd Can you follow up with L3? Lots cannot be mixed on WIP pallets to avoid mix ups on the FFS.
QA will be putting this super sack on hold/rework.

Rose Smuin,

Would you please follow up with your teams?

We can’t mix lots in supersacks.
Mathew Draper #Ahtziri Arteaga
We will place it on hold and then proceed with the verification; it appears we have two lots: 61BUC-1 and 61AUC-3.

Janeth is working on the list of the area that didn’t passed. After talking to her an walking what didn’t passed where the cooling tunnels and some belts. Once I get the list I’ll give it to Raoul.

We are working on a plan for tonight to have a team cleaning and do the test on the areas that fail to make sure the line is ready for Monday Raoul, Rose Chad Alan Preston

Alan Greer,

Did we do something different tonight where we weren’t able to get the level 2 clean complete?

Alan Greer 
 
Please have a team come in tonight. 
 
All lines need an on time start Monday.
 
Thank you
No, we weren’t able to finish the cleaning. We will be here tonight to re clean and make sure the line is ready for Monday

We need to evaluate the cleans and how they were performed in L1. I observed it takes around 6 hours to complete Level 1 the last two weeks. Today our main guy Jonathan who has the most experience had the day off. After speaking with Chris the backup for Jonathan he told me the level 2 usually takes around 9 hours.

Agreed. 

They consistently executed this in 8 hours in L1 Frederick Peairs, Alan Greer
There are some changes as we moved over so we need the team to assess to ensure we have the right standards and create improvements as well

Chad Stone/
Good morning, no we didn’t do anything different. We completed the level 2 but QA did not pass sections on the Cavanna and  the North tunnel. When I got back from the meeting several employees had left the plant. We needed to run the north belt in order to complete ATP.
Only an hour hour and a half are needed to re clean the failed tested areas
Sounds great!

When over with Luisiana, and Raoul about the area that needs to be reclean. Posted on the whiteboard the areas that need the ATP. As soon as the clean is finish and QA take the swabs we would post again
The pipes are working properly
Level 2 clean completed and passed off ATP

Raoul Lubain 
Thank you
The chain has been running well
@Ethan Bean The two rolls of fw found on the line, the smaller one, from the Bosch according to EE that was cleaning the Bosch yesterday
Are those rolls good or do they have any defects?

The tempek chiller for the kitchen is making a loud noise that I haven't heard before, and there is also what appears to be water leaking from underneath it. We checked all the temperatures in the kitchen HMI as well as the caramel temperature in the caramel hopper, and they all look OK. Maintenance has been informed, and will take a look at it asap.

We may have to slow down the line within the next hour or two to around 50rr to be able to catch up. Cooking time for C2 has been drastically increasing in the last few hours, making it hard to keep up with the production rr.


Katya Farnsworth , I'm aware 3rd shift did a caustic wash, but at this rate 2nd shift won't be able to run for too long without a caustic wash due to Cooking times. Just a heads up.

Alex is confident he can get the errors cleared today. We won't run any solid bars today. Yesterday, TG#3 was running at 900 PPM, and TG#4 was at 1000PPM. We plan to start with solid bars on Monday so Theegarten can dial in TG#5.
These box machine parts were cleaned and successfully passed the visual inspection and ATP test.
WDS is now running at 5mpm recirculating, Alex went to lunch, when he comes back we will speed up gradually to check there’s no more issues.
